events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need parse scenario. It's a hazard check: Housekeeping & general hazard recognition commercial project. Scenario describes open-ended "find all hazards in this jobsite photo" item - cords across walkways, missing guardrails, blocked egress/extinguishers, improvised scaffolds, unattended open holes. Work actively in progress. Need identify safety hazards, severity, immediate controls. Decision must be fail because dangerous/non-compliant. Risk likely critical due to multiple imminent hazards (fall, egress, fire, electrocution). component_conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 50? value 0. sound_source irrelevant. confidence 0.95. event_time 0. rate 0. order empty. workable empty. findings list hazards. actions list immediate controls. rationale. references OSHA general duty clause, 29 CFR 1926 Subpart L, plus maybe 1926.20 housekeeping, 1926.21, 1926.451, 1926.452, 1926.501 fall protection, 1926.403 electrical, 1926.150 fire protection, 1926.34 egress. Need include only JSON. Ensure valid JSON. Let's craft.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": [
    "installed-defective",
    "non-compliant"
  ],
  "percent_complete": 50,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Extension cords and leads routed across walkways, creating trip and electrical hazards",
    "Missing or incomplete guardrails at elevated edges and openings",
    "Egress paths blocked by materials, debris, or equipment",
    "Fire extinguishers blocked, obscured, or inaccessible",
    "Improvised scaffolds or platforms not erected per approved design and Subpart L requirements",
    "Unattended open holes and floor openings without covers or guarding"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove or reroute cords from walkways; use overhead runs, cord covers, or protected raceways; inspect cords for damage and GFCI protection",
    "Install compliant guardrails, mid-rails, toeboards, or equivalent fall protection at all open edges, holes, and elevated platforms immediately",
    "Clear all egress routes and ensure exits and fire extinguishers are visible, accessible, and unobstructed",
    "Dismantle or have a competent person re-inspect and approve any improvised scaffolds before use; ensure footings, planks, access, and guardrails meet Subpart L",
    "Cover or guard all unattended floor holes and openings with secured, labeled covers capable of supporting intended loads",
    "Conduct an immediate site safety sweep, retrain workers, and restrict access to affected areas until hazards are corrected"
  ],
  "rationale": "The observed conditions present multiple imminent dangers: fall-from-elevation, trip, electrical shock, fire/life-safety egress, and scaffold collapse hazards. These violate OSHA general duty and 1926 Subpart L scaffold requirements, requiring immediate abatement and a stop-work/fail determination.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA General Duty Clause (29 U.S.C. 654(a)(1))",
    "29 CFR 1926 Subpart L (Scaffolds)",
    "29 CFR 1926.20 / 1926.25 (Housekeeping and accident prevention)",
    "29 CFR 1926.34 (Access to exits)",
    "29 CFR 1926.150 (Fire protection)",
    "29 CFR 1926.403 (Electrical)",
    "29 CFR 1926.501 (Fall protection)"
  ]
}
